title: DeepSeek-V4: Towards Highly Efficient Million-Token Context Intelligence
summary:
arXiv:2606.19348v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present a preview version of DeepSeek-V4 series, including two strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models -- DeepSeek-V4-Pro with 1.6T parameters (49B activated) and DeepSeek-V4-Flash with 284B parameters (13B activated) -- both supporting a context length of one million tokens. DeepSeek-V4 series incorporate several key upgrades in architecture and optimization: (1) a hybrid attention architecture that combines Compressed Sparse Attention (CSA) and Heavily Compressed Attention (HCA) to improve long-context efficiency; (2) Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections (mHC) that enhance conventional residual connections; (3) and the Muon optimizer for faster convergence and greater training stability. We pre-train both models on more than 32T diverse and high-quality tokens, followed by a comprehensive post-training pipeline that unlocks and further enhances their capabilities. DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Max, the maximum reasoning effort mode of DeepSeek-V4-Pro, redefines the state-of-the-art for open models, outperforming its predecessors in core tasks. Meanwhile, DeepSeek-V4 series are highly efficient in long-context scenarios. In the one-million-token context setting, DeepSeek-V4-Pro requires only 27% of single-token inference FLOPs and 10% of KV cache compared with DeepSeek-V3.2. This enables us to routinely support one-million-token contexts, thereby making long-horizon tasks and further test-time scaling more feasible. The model checkpoints are available at https://huggingface.co/collections/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4.
title_es: DeepSeek-V4: modelos MoE con contexto de un millón de tokens
bajada: DeepSeek presenta su serie V4 con dos modelos de expertos mezclados que soportan contexto de un millón de tokens. Las nuevas arquitecturas incluyen atención híbrida y optimizaciones que mejoran significativamente la eficiencia en escenarios de largo contexto.
bajada_en: DeepSeek introduces its V4 series with two mixture-of-experts models supporting one million token context. New architectures include hybrid attention and optimizations that significantly improve efficiency in long-context scenarios.

title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
summary: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. But Subquadratic has started to bring the receipts, sharing the…
title_es: Startup Subquadratic afirma resolver cuello de botella en LLMs
bajada: La startup Subquadratic salió del sigilo asegurando haber resuelto un bottleneck matemático que limitaba a los LLMs hace casi una década. Aunque los detalles aún son escasos, la empresa está presentando evidencia de su breakthrough.
bajada_en: Subquadratic emerged from stealth claiming to have solved a mathematical bottleneck constraining LLMs for nearly a decade. While details remain sparse, the company is presenting evidence of its breakthrough.

title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
summary: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails.  Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is […]
title_es: Gobierno retira modelos de Anthropic por preocupaciones de seguridad
bajada: El gobierno estadounidense obligó a Anthropic a retirar Fable 5 y Mythos 5 tras hallazgos de vulnerabilidades de seguridad. La medida ha generado debate entre investigadores sobre su efectividad, señalando que problemas similares existen en otros modelos.
bajada_en: The US government forced Anthropic to withdraw Fable 5 and Mythos 5 following security vulnerability findings. The measure has sparked debate among researchers about its effectiveness, noting similar issues exist in other models.

title: Siri AI Hands On: A Smart, Helpful Assistant
summary: The new Siri AI is conversational, omnipresent, and actually helpful.
title_es: Apple mejora Siri con capacidades conversacionales de IA
bajada: El nuevo Siri integra IA conversacional haciéndolo más útil y omnipresente en dispositivos Apple. La herramienta mejora significativamente su capacidad de asistencia al usuario.
bajada_en: New Siri integrates conversational AI making it more useful and omnipresent across Apple devices. The assistant significantly improves its user assistance capabilities.

title: Norway imposes near ban on AI in elementary school
title_es: Noruega impone restricciones severas de IA en escuelas primarias
bajada: Noruega implementó restricciones cercanas a una prohibición del uso de IA en educación primaria. La medida refleja crecientes preocupaciones sobre el impacto de la IA en el aprendizaje infantil.
bajada_en: Norway implemented restrictions near-banning AI use in primary education. The measure reflects growing concerns about AI's impact on childhood learning.

title: DeusData /codebase-memory-mcp
summary: codebase-memory-mcp The fastest and most efficient code intelligence engine for AI coding agents. Full-indexes an average repository in milliseconds, the Linux kernel (28M LOC, 75K files) in 3 minutes. Answers structural queries in under 1ms. Ships as a single static binary for macOS, Linux, and Windows — download, run install , done. High-quality parsing through tree-sitter AST analysis across all 158 languages, enhanced with Hybrid LSP semantic type resolution for Python, TypeScript / JavaScript / JSX / TSX, PHP, C#, Go, C, C++, Java, Kotlin, and Rust — producing a persistent knowledge graph of functions, classes, call chains, HTTP routes, and cross-service links. 14 MCP tools. Zero dependencies. Plug and play across 11 coding agents. Research — The design and benchmarks behind this project are described in the preprint Codebase-Memory: Tree-Sitter-Based Knowledge Graphs for LLM Code Exploration via MCP (arXiv:2603.27277). Evaluated across 31 real-world repositories: 83% answer quality, 10× fewer tokens, 2.1× fewer tool calls vs. file-by-file exploration. Security & Trust — This tool reads your codebase and writes to your agent configuration files. That is what it is designed to do. If you prefer to audit before running, the full source is here — every release binary is signed, checksummed, and scanned by 70+ antivirus engines. All processing happens 100% locally; your code never leaves your machine. Found a security issue? We want to know — see SECURITY.md . Security is Priority #1 for us. Built-in 3D graph visualization (UI variant) — explore your knowledge graph at localhost:9749 Why codebase-memory-mcp Extreme indexing speed — Linux kernel (28M LOC, 75K files) in 3 minutes. RAM-first pipeline: LZ4 compression, in-memory SQLite, fused Aho-Corasick pattern matching. Memory released after indexing. Plug and play — single static binary for macOS (arm64/amd64), Linux (arm64/amd64), and Windows (amd64). No Docker, no runtime dependencies, no API keys. Download → ins
title_es: codebase-memory-mcp: motor de inteligencia de código para agentes IA
bajada: DeusData lanzó codebase-memory-mcp, una herramienta que indexa repositorios en milisegundos usando análisis AST. Funciona como agente MCP con 14 herramientas integradas, compatible con 11 agentes de código diferentes.
bajada_en: DeusData launched codebase-memory-mcp, a tool that indexes repositories in milliseconds using AST analysis. Works as an MCP agent with 14 integrated tools, compatible with 11 different coding agents.

title: GPT-5.5 hallucinates 3x more than MIT-licensed GLM-5.2
title_es: GPT-5.5 genera 3x más alucinaciones que GLM-5.2
bajada: Un análisis comparativo muestra que GPT-5.5 produce significativamente más alucinaciones que el modelo de licencia MIT GLM-5.2. Los resultados cuestionan la relación entre tamaño del modelo y confiabilidad.
bajada_en: A comparative analysis shows GPT-5.5 produces significantly more hallucinations than the MIT-licensed GLM-5.2 model. Results question the relationship between model size and reliability.

title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
summary: Reliance is weaving AI into telecom services used by more than 500 million people.
title_es: Reliance integra IA en servicios de telecom para 500M usuarios
bajada: El multimillonario Ambani planea integrar IA en llamadas, apps y hogares a través de Reliance. La iniciativa busca llevar capacidades de IA a más de 500 millones de usuarios de telecom en India.
bajada_en: Billionaire Ambani plans to integrate AI into calls, apps and homes through Reliance. The initiative aims to bring AI capabilities to over 500 million telecom users in India.

title: Companies rein in AI usage as costs strain budgets
title_es: Empresas reducen inversión en IA por presión de costos
bajada: Compañías están limitando el uso de IA a medida que los costos de infraestructura y modelos afectan presupuestos. La presión económica impulsa decisiones más selectivas sobre dónde implementar tecnología de IA.
bajada_en: Companies are curtailing AI usage as infrastructure and model costs strain budgets. Economic pressure drives more selective decisions about where to deploy AI technology.

title: An open handbook on LLM inference at scale (GPU internals, KV cache, batching, vLLM/SGLang/TensorRT-LLM) [P]
summary: I've been working through the internals of LLM inference and writing up what I learn as an open, in-progress handbook.Just wrapped another chapter on GPU execution and memory internals: why a GPU sits mostly idle during inference, how the memory hierarchy gates throughput, and where the real bottlenecks live. Added mermaid diagrams for the architecture pieces so the flow is easier to follow than a wall of text.It's a personal learning project, still growing chapter by chapter. I'd value feedback or corrections from anyone who's run inference in production, where my mental model breaks down is exactly what I want to find. Issues and PRs welcome.github.com/harshuljain13/llm-inference-at-scalesubmitted by/u/YouFirst295[link][comments]
title_es: Manual abierto sobre inferencia de LLMs a escala
bajada: Un desarrollador publicó un handbook gratuito y en construcción sobre internals de inferencia de LLMs cubriendo GPU, KV cache y batching. Es un proyecto educativo con diagramas y bienvenida a contribuciones de la comunidad.
bajada_en: A developer published a free, in-progress handbook on LLM inference internals covering GPU, KV cache and batching. It's an educational project with diagrams and welcomes community contributions.

title: DVD-JEPA: an open-source, fully-reproducible JEPA world model [P]
summary: A paper currently trending onpaperswithcode.coin the "Anomaly Detection" category isDVD-JEPA.https://i.redd.it/r6fd8n3d4f8h1.gifHere is the short summary:Most attempts to learn aworld modelfrom video try to predict the next frame pixel-by-pixel, and drown in detail that is fundamentally unpredictable.JEPA(Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture,LeCun 2022) makes a different bet: predict therepresentationof the future, not the pixels, and let the encoder discard whatever it cannot predict.DVD-JEPAis the smallest honest demonstration of that idea we could build. The "world" is a DVD logo bouncing in a 16×16 box. A context encoder, an EMA target encoder, and a latent predictor are trained — with no labels and no decoder — to predict the next observationin a 32-dimensional representation space. We then show three things:It learned the world.A linear probe recovers the logo's exact (y, x) position from the frozen 32-d latent to within0.73 px— though it was never given a coordinate.It can dream (once you add a decoder).Bolt an optional decoder onto the frozen latents and roll the predictor forward: it renders a correctfuture-frame videoof the bounce, including wall reflections, for ~20 steps before latent drift sets in.It is useful.Run it as a 1-step predictive monitor, and the prediction error becomes ananomaly signal: inject a teleport and surprise spikes88×over baseline, on the right frame.The whole thing runsclient-side in your browser— the trained MLPs are re-implemented in ~40 lines of JavaScript. It is a joke, and it is also a correct, working instance of the architecture behind I-JEPA, V-JEPA, and V-JEPA 2.Find the paper, HF model, and project page here:https://paperswithcode.co/paper/98361submitted by/u/NielsRogge[link][comments]
title_es: DVD-JEPA: modelo de mundo JEPA reproducible y open-source
bajada: Investigadores lanzaron DVD-JEPA, una demostración de arquitectura JEPA para aprender modelos de mundo a partir de video. El modelo aprende representaciones futuras en lugar de predecir píxeles, con demostración exitosa en video sintético.
bajada_en: Researchers released DVD-JEPA, a JEPA architecture demonstration for learning world models from video. The model learns future representations instead of predicting pixels, with successful demonstration on synthetic video.

title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
summary: Five months after returning to OpenAI, Barret Zoph - the company's head of enterprise AI sales - has departed, The Verge has learned. Zoph returned to OpenAI in mid-January after a stint as co-founder and CTO of Thinking Machines Lab, the competing AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati. Shortly after Zoph returned […]
title_es: Barret Zoph abandona OpenAI nuevamente tras cinco meses
bajada: Barret Zoph, quien regresó a OpenAI como head de ventas enterprise hace cinco meses, ha dejado la compañía nuevamente. Zoph fue cofundador y CTO de Thinking Machines Lab, compañía competidora fundada por Mira Murati.
bajada_en: Barret Zoph, who returned to OpenAI as head of enterprise sales five months ago, has left the company again. Zoph was co-founder and CTO of Thinking Machines Lab, a competing company founded by Mira Murati.

title: Is AI ruining our skills? Early results are in – and they're not good
title_es: Primeros resultados: ¿está deteriorando la IA nuestras habilidades?
bajada: Investigación publicada en Nature examina si el uso de IA afecta negativamente el desarrollo de habilidades profesionales. Los primeros resultados sugieren impactos preocupantes en retención de conocimiento y práctica deliberada.
bajada_en: Research published in Nature examines whether AI use negatively affects professional skill development. Early results suggest concerning impacts on knowledge retention and deliberate practice.

title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
summary: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.
title_es: Controles de exportación de IA: lecciones de 30 años de fracaso
bajada: Un análisis histórico compara los controles de exportación de tecnología de ciberseguridad con las restricciones actuales sobre modelos como Mythos. La evidencia sugiere que estos controles han sido inefectivos históricamente.
bajada_en: A historical analysis compares export controls on cybersecurity technology with current restrictions on models like Mythos. Evidence suggests these controls have been historically ineffective.

title: The Download: AI bottleneck debates, and BCI trials take off
summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth last month with a huge claim: it had solved a mathematical bottleneck…
title_es: Debates sobre bottlenecks en LLMs y avances en BCIs
bajada: MIT Tech Review resume los últimos desarrollos: debates sobre limitaciones arquitectónicas en LLMs y progresos en ensayos de interfaces cerebro-computadora. Dos tendencias clave que definen el mes en IA.
bajada_en: MIT Tech Review summarizes recent developments: debates on architectural limitations in LLMs and progress in brain-computer interface trials. Two key trends defining the month in AI.
